# Nuclear Weapons

> **Sumerian name:** NAM.ḪUL (𒉆𒃲) — "Weapons of Terror"

Zecharia Sitchin proposed that the Anunnaki possessed and used nuclear weapons on Earth. The most significant event in his narrative involving nuclear weapons is the destruction of [Sippar](../places/Sippar.md) and [Ur](../places/Ur.md) in 2024 BCE — a catastrophe that, according to Sitchin, ended the Sumerian civilization and forced the Anunnaki to abandon Earth.

## The Nuclear Strike of 2024 BCE

Sitchin identified the destruction of the Sumerian city of Ur (c. 2004 BCE by conventional dating) as a nuclear event:

> *"The evil wind, the terrible storm, came upon the land. The cities were destroyed; the people were turned to stone."* — From the Sumerian lamentations

Evidence Sitchin cited:

1. **Simultaneous destruction** — Multiple Sumerian cities were destroyed at roughly the same time
2. **The "evil wind"** — Sumerian texts describe a poisonous cloud that brought death
3. **Soil samples** — Reports of high radioactivity in soil layers corresponding to this period
4. **Mass graves** — The "death pits" of Ur containing hundreds of simultaneous burials
5. **Biblical parallels** — The destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah by "brimstone and fire"

## The Target: Sippar Spaceport

According to Sitchin, the primary target of the nuclear strike was [Sippar](../places/Sippar.md), the Anunnaki spaceport. [Enlil](../characters/Enlil.md) and [Ninurta](../characters/Ninurta.md) authorized the strike to prevent [Marduk](../characters/Marduk.md) and his forces from seizing control of the spaceport and using it to establish supremacy over the other Anunnaki.

## The Weapon

Sitchin did not specify the exact nature of the weapon, but suggested it was a form of nuclear or directed-energy weapon:

- The "weapon of the great ones" mentioned in Sumerian texts
- Possibly a nuclear warhead delivered by a missile or aircraft
- The "scorched earth" effect described in the texts

> *"The weapon used against Sippar was not merely a conventional explosive. It was a weapon of mass destruction that left a lasting mark on the land — a mark that can still be measured by modern instruments."*

## Nergal and the "Evil Wind"

Sitchin connected the god [Nergal](../characters/Nergal.md) with the nuclear strike. Nergal's association with plague, destruction, and the "evil wind" made him the natural figure to deliver or control the nuclear weapon.
